message:
fix(core): eliminate race condition in now_micros() monotonic clock

The previous implementation used a relaxed load+store pair on the
LAST_SYSTEM_TIME_US AtomicI64. Two concurrent threads could both read
the same `last` value, both decide their `current` was newer, and both
store — with the slower thread's earlier timestamp overwriting the
faster thread's later one, breaking the monotonicity guarantee.

Replace with AtomicI64::fetch_max (a single atomic read-modify-write)
which atomically stores max(old, new) and returns the previous value.
This eliminates the TOCTOU window entirely. Also upgrade ordering from
Relaxed to AcqRel for the high-water mark operations to ensure proper
happens-before relationships across threads.

Additionally, guarantee strict monotonicity (no duplicate values): when
the clock stands still or goes backward, return prev + 1 and push that
into the high-water mark with a second fetch_max.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
